title: RadarMosaicRecipe
description:
Sentinel-1 SAR backscatter mosaic. S1 = ESA C-band SAR (S1A/S1B); sees through clouds, rain, night — only practical sensor for cloud-prone regions and time-critical work (floods, deforestation alerts).

Output in **dB**. Typical land surface: VV roughly -20…0 dB (forest -8…-12, urban 0…+5, water -20…-25); VH roughly -25…-10 (always lower than VV).

Bands — point-in-time: VV, VH, ratio_VV_VH, orbit, dayOfYear, daysFromTarget. Time-scan: per-polarization aggregates (min/mean/median/max/stdDev), VV/VH ratio bands, NDCV.

This schema describes the persisted `model` of an initialized recipe. Cross-field constraints (date order, S1 epoch, multitemporal-requires-spatial, incidence-angle ordering) live in companion rules.js.

aoi.description: Area of Interest. EE table or polygon — `ASSET_BOUNDS` not valid here (radar mosaic doesn't wrap a single asset). User must provide; no default.

dates.title: RadarMosaicDates
dates.description:
Two modes:

**Point-in-time** `{targetDate}` — composites scenes near `targetDate` (S1 revisit ~6–12 days at most latitudes). Scene window: ±15d when `outlierRemoval='NONE'` else ±183d (wider window needed for stable outlier stats). Use for: visualizing a specific date, change detection at known timestamps.

**Time-scan** `{fromDate, toDate}` — composites every scene in half-open `[fromDate, toDate)`. **`toDate` is exclusive** — full year 2024 is `{fromDate:'2024-01-01', toDate:'2025-01-01'}`, January is `{fromDate:'2024-01-01', toDate:'2024-02-01'}` (NOT `2024-01-31`). Produces per-polarization aggregate bands. Use for: temporal characterization, long-term stats, time-series classification training.

S1 coverage from 2014-06-15 (S1A launched 04/2014). S1A+S1B: 2016–late 2021; after S1B failure (12/2021) revisit ~12d at equator; S1C launched 12/2024. High latitudes and some Pacific islands sparse.

targetDate.description: Center date for point-in-time mosaic. Window ±15d (`outlierRemoval='NONE'`) or ±183d. Must be in [2014-06-15, today].

fromDate.description: Inclusive start of time-scan interval. Min useful 2014-06-15 (S1 epoch); earlier dates have no scenes. Must be ≥ 2014-01-01, ≤ today, < toDate.

toDate.description: Exclusive end of time-scan interval — set to day AFTER last covered day. Full year 2024: `2025-01-01` (NOT `2024-12-31`). January 2024: `2024-02-01` (NOT `2024-01-31`). Must be > 2014-06-15 (to overlap S1 data), > fromDate, ≤ start of year after current year (future allowed for mid-year yearly aggregates).

options.title: RadarMosaicOptions
options.description: SAR processing options. Defaults tuned for general land monitoring at moderate scales. Many fields are conditional — see per-field. Key interactions: speckle filtering is two-stage (spatial filter optionally + temporal filter; temporal requires spatial); TERRAIN geometric correction substantially increases EE cost but is essential in mountainous terrain (without it, slopes facing the satellite appear bright, away dark, regardless of land cover); `SIDES` border masking depends on `minAngle`/`maxAngle`.

advanced.description: UI hint, no processing effect. Omit.

orbits.description:
S1 orbit directions (asc = south→north, desc = north→south); both available over most regions, doubling scene count.
- `['ASCENDING','DESCENDING']` (default) — max scene count, best for non-directional analysis.
- single direction — for change detection or polarimetric work needing consistent look geometry / incidence-angle distribution.

orbitNumbers.description:
How to handle multiple relative-orbit tracks over the AOI. Adjacent tracks overlap at different incidence angles → seams when combined naively.
- `ALL` (default) — every track. Best signal-to-noise; possible seam artifacts at orbit boundaries.
- `DOMINANT` — only the largest-overlap track per direction. No seams; fewer observations; possible AOI-edge gaps. **Use for time-scans where seams distort statistics.**
- `ADJACENT` — dominant + neighbours. Backend currently falls back to DOMINANT.

**Use ALL for point-in-time** (every observation helps), **DOMINANT for time-scans**.

geometricCorrection.description:
σ⁰ → γ⁰ correction: makes pixels comparable across look geometries.
- `NONE` — raw σ⁰. Cheapest; debugging only.
- `ELLIPSOID` — γ⁰ via WGS84 ellipsoid incidence angle. Removes near-vs-far range cosine effect. Suitable for flat terrain (flood plains, plateaus, low-relief agriculture).
- `TERRAIN` (default) — flat-earth γ⁰ via GLO-30 (Copernicus DEM, 30 m, global except >85° lat). Removes look-angle AND topographic effect. **Essential in mountainous/hilly terrain** — without it, terrain dominates the signal.

TERRAIN is materially more expensive (DEM lookup, slope/aspect, layover/shadow masking) but almost always worth it.

spatialSpeckleFilter.description:
Per-scene spatial speckle filter (SAR speckle is multiplicative salt-and-pepper noise inherent to coherent imaging).
- `NONE` — no spatial filter. Max detail, max speckle. Reasonable when downstream zonal stats over large polygons or heavy temporal averaging will smooth.
- `GAMMA_MAP` — MAP filter, gamma-distributed reflectivity (Lopes 1990). Moderate cost.
- `LEE` — classic Lee MMSE (1980). Fast; can blur high-contrast edges.
- `REFINED_LEE` — direction-aware Lee, 8-direction gradient detection. Best edge preservation among classic filters; **~5× more expensive**; fixed 7×7 kernel (`kernelSize` ignored).
- `LEE_SIGMA` (default) — Lee with adaptive sigma rejection (2009). Good edge preservation; controlled by `sigma`.
- `SNIC` — superpixel segmentation. Strongest noise reduction; loses fine detail; segmentation artifacts. **Most expensive — visual products only.**

Guidance: LEE_SIGMA for general use; REFINED_LEE if edges critical and AOI small; NONE if heavy `multitemporalSpeckleFilter` or downstream zonal stats; SNIC for visualization only.

kernelSize.description:
Filter kernel side in pixels (odd, 3–25). 9 = 9×9 = 81 px. S1 GRD pixels are 10 m → kernel 9 is ~90 m window. Larger = more smoothing, more edge blur.
- 3, 5 — minimal smoothing; preserves forest gaps, small clearings; high residual speckle.
- 7, 9 (default 9) — balanced.
- 11–15 — stronger; visual products, homogeneous land cover; blurs small features.
- 17–25 — heavy; for downstream smoothed inputs; merges small features.

Required when `spatialSpeckleFilter` ∈ {GAMMA_MAP, LEE, LEE_SIGMA}. Ignored for NONE, REFINED_LEE (fixed 7×7), SNIC (uses `snicSize`). EE cost ~ kernel area.

sigma.description:
Lee Sigma rejection fraction (`spatialSpeckleFilter='LEE_SIGMA'` only). Controls outlier rejection from local mean. Lower = sharper edges, less smoothing; higher = smoother, more edge blur.
- 0.5 — most aggressive; max edge preservation, least smoothing.
- 0.6/0.7/0.8 — increasing smoothness.
- 0.9 (default) — balanced.
- 0.95 — most conservative; max speckle reduction; some edge blur.

Discrete enum — backend uses hardcoded lookup table; arbitrary values not accepted.

strongScatterers.description:
How to handle strong scatterers (corner reflectors, urban structures, certain vegetation) — high backscatter is real signal but dominates local stats, blurring edges around them.
- `RETAIN` (default) — detect via `strongScattererValues`, exclude from filter input, re-insert original values. Preserves point-target intensity at small extra cost.
- `FILTER` — smooth them like ordinary pixels. Visual products only, when you don't care about point-target intensity.

Required when `spatialSpeckleFilter` ≠ NONE.

strongScattererValues.description:
`[VV-threshold, VH-threshold]` in dB. Pixel = strong scatterer iff VV > VV-threshold AND VH > VH-threshold (compared in linear power, 10^(threshold/10)).

Default `[0, -5]` catches corner reflectors and urban targets but not normal land cover (forest VV ~-8…-12, cropland VV ~-10…-14, cropland VH ~-16…-20). Lowering catches more (over-detection: ordinary bright pixels skipped by filter). Raising catches fewer (under-detection: real strong scatterers get smoothed). Range [-30, 30] dB. Used when `strongScatterers='RETAIN'`.

snicSize.description:
SNIC seed spacing in pixels. Larger = coarser superpixels, more smoothing.
- 3 — fine; preserves boundaries.
- 5 (default) — moderate.
- 7–15 — increasingly coarse; for highly homogeneous land cover; loses small features.

Required when `spatialSpeckleFilter='SNIC'`.

snicCompactness.description:
SNIC spatial-vs-spectral weighting (`spatialSpeckleFilter='SNIC'` only). Higher = rounder/squarer superpixels at cost of spectral homogeneity; lower = follows spectral boundaries (irregular shapes).
- 0 — pure spectral; noisy irregular shapes following speckle.
- 0.15 (default) — slight spatial preference.
- 0.4/0.7/1/1.5/2 — increasingly spatial (2 ≈ square regardless of content).

Range [0, 2].

multitemporalSpeckleFilter.description:
Multi-temporal speckle filter across scenes — exploits speckle independence between acquisitions to dramatically reduce noise without spatial blur.
- `NONE` (default).
- `QUEGAN` — Quegan & Yu 2001. Estimates per-pixel speckle ratio from `numberOfImages` time-series scenes, divides each filtered scene by it. Excellent noise reduction, edge preservation. Useful for time-scans over stable land cover.
- `RABASAR` — divides each scene by 'super-image' temporal mean, spatially filters the (locally-stationary) ratio, multiplies back. Quegan variant; **handles change events better.**

**Constraint:** must be `NONE` when `spatialSpeckleFilter='NONE'` (rules.js enforces).

**Very expensive** — joins each scene with `numberOfImages` neighbours, computes per-pixel temporal stats. Cost ~ `numberOfImages × scene_count`; on yearly time-scan (hundreds of S1 scenes) with default `numberOfImages=10` this is the dominant compute cost — retrievals stretch from minutes to hours. **Leave at NONE unless user explicitly asks for temporal/multitemporal/Quegan filtering or stronger noise reduction than spatial-only.**

numberOfImages.description:
Scenes used by multi-temporal filter. Variance ~1/N; cost scales linearly.
- 2–5 — modest noise reduction; when scenes scarce.
- 10 (default) — good reduction, reasonable cost.
- 20–50 — strong; stable land cover only.
- >50 — diminishing returns; very long stationary series.

**Caveat:** filter assumes statistically similar scenes — biased estimates around deforestation events, floods, etc. For dynamic landscapes, prefer fewer images closer in time. Required when both speckle filters non-NONE.

outlierRemoval.description:
Temporal outlier rejection: per-pixel temporal median + stdDev, mask >Nσ.
- `NONE` — keep all observations (preserves transient events: floods, fires).
- `MODERATE` (default) — >3σ.
- `AGGRESSIVE` — >2.6σ; suppresses some real variability.
- `CONSERVATIVE` — >4σ. Backend supports it but GUI doesn't expose — only pick if user explicitly asks.

When NONE, point-in-time window is ±15d (30d total); else ±183d (366d, needed for stable temporal stats).

mask.description:
Edge masks per scene.
- `SIDES` — keep pixels with incidence angle in [`minAngle`, `maxAngle`]. Removes oblique swath-edge pixels where geometric effects dominate.
- `FIRST_LAST` — drop first/last azimuth slices of each orbit pass (unreliable processing artifacts at acquisition start/end).

Default `['SIDES','FIRST_LAST']`. Empty `[]` keeps all pixels including unreliable ones.

minAngle.description:
Min local incidence angle (degrees) for `SIDES` masking. S1 GRD swath ~29°–46°. Pixels below are near-range; backscatter values unusually high and unreliable.

Default 30.88 (empirical near-range boundary). Lowering keeps more (lower-quality) pixels; raising discards more. Used when `mask` includes `'SIDES'`. Must be < `maxAngle` (rules.js).

maxAngle.description:
Max local incidence angle (degrees) for `SIDES` masking. Pixels above are far-range — weaker signal, more sensitive to small geometric variations.

Default 45.35 (empirical far-range boundary). Used when `mask` includes `'SIDES'`. Must be > `minAngle`.

minObservations.description:
Min valid scenes for a pixel to appear in output. Pixels below are masked.
- 1 (default) — max coverage; some pixels may rest on a single noisy obs.
- 2–5 — small redundancy; excludes too-sparsely-sampled pixels.
- 10 — strong redundancy; good for time-scans where temporal stats matter.
- >20 — strict; only for areas with dense S1 coverage and long intervals.

Range [1, 50].

allOf.description: kernelSize required when spatialSpeckleFilter is GAMMA_MAP, LEE, or LEE_SIGMA.
sigma required when spatialSpeckleFilter is LEE_SIGMA.
snicSize and snicCompactness required when spatialSpeckleFilter is SNIC.
strongScatterers required when spatialSpeckleFilter is not NONE.
strongScattererValues required when strongScatterers is RETAIN.
numberOfImages required when both speckle filters are non-NONE.
minAngle and maxAngle required when mask includes 'SIDES'.
